codeReview.summary: The prior blocking bioRxiv/medRxiv issue is resolved: the connectors now construct provider-style query searches against the public search pages and the regression tests cover older and query-based matches. I did not find any remaining blocking defects in the fix, but one pre-existing capability-metadata mismatch remains.

issues.description: The canonical source registry still reports several first-wave sources as `download`/`read` `supported` (for example arXiv at lines 90 and bioRxiv at line 92, with the same pattern for medRxiv, CORE, IACR, PMC, and Europe PMC), but their connector methods still return explicit `unsupported` results (`internal/connectors/first_wave.go`: 152-156, 195-199, 231-235, 504-508, 620-624, 749-753, 875-879). This keeps `sources` output inconsistent with actual runtime retrieval behavior for those providers.

summary: Review passed. The fix replaces the last-30-days category approximation with provider-style bioRxiv/medRxiv query search and adds regression coverage for older and author/query-based matches. The only remaining issue I found is the existing non-blocking mismatch between registry capability metadata and current retrieval implementation for several first-wave sources.
